title: Classify Visual Studio Build Errors
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that reads Visual Studio error codes and prints which configuration step was most likely missed.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 20).
- Lines 2..N+1: one error code per line. Codes are one of: `C1083`, `LNK2019`, `LNK1104`, `LNK1112`, `RUNTIME`.

## Output
For each error code, print the likely missed step:
- `C1083` → `missing include directory`
- `LNK2019` → `missing additional dependency`
- `LNK1104` → `missing library directory`
- `LNK1112` → `architecture mismatch`
- `RUNTIME` → `missing dll`

## Requirements
1. Each result on its own line.
2. N=1 must work for any valid code.
3. All five codes must map to the correct message exactly as shown above.

test_cases.label: Sample: three errors
test_cases.expected_stdout:
missing include directory
missing additional dependency
missing dll

title: Library Configuration Validator
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `isConfigComplete` that checks whether all three Visual Studio library configuration steps have been performed.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10), the number of configurations to check.
- For each configuration: three lines, each containing `yes` or `no`, representing:
  - Whether include directory was added
  - Whether library directory was added
  - Whether additional dependency was added

## Output
For each configuration, print `complete` if all three are `yes`, otherwise print `incomplete`.

## Requirements
1. All three settings must be `yes` for the result to be `complete`.
2. Any single `no` makes the configuration `incomplete`.
3. Print one result per line.

test_cases.label: Sample: complete then incomplete
test_cases.stdin:
2
yes
yes
yes
yes
no
yes

test_cases.label: Hidden: all incomplete
test_cases.stdin:
3
no
no
no
yes
yes
no
no
yes
yes
